steps.title: Recognize that adding the equations eliminates y
steps.content: Notice that the first equation has $+5y$ and the second has $-5y$. When we add these equations together, the $y$ terms will cancel, leaving us with an equation in only $x$. This is the fastest path to the answer.

steps.title: Add the equations and solve for x
steps.content:
Adding equation 1 and equation 2:
$$(x + 5y) + (x - 5y) = 5 + 7$$
$$2x = 12$$
$$x = 6$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
